rationale: The citing paper uses Novemsky and Kahneman's intentions account as a key theoretical input in Experiment 3, not just as background. It explicitly argues that expected exchange should weaken loss-aversion coding and then builds a moderator manipulation around that claim to test whether its time-perception effect has loss-aversion-like properties. The paper also interprets its interaction results through this intentions-based lens, citing Novemsky and Kahneman (2005a,b) as the basis for that interpretation.
relation_description: Bilgin and LeBoeuf apply the intentions-based loss-aversion logic from Novemsky and Kahneman to a new domain, future time perception. They use expected versus unexpected exchange as a diagnostic moderator to show their looming-losses effect behaves similarly to loss aversion.
